phase_metadata.title: HTML Links and Media
phase_metadata.description:
Connecting pages through hyperlinks and enriching content with
images, audio, and video.

learning_objectives: Create hyperlinks to internal and external web pages
Understand the difference between relative and absolute URLs

Embed and
optimize images with proper accessibility descriptions

Implement audio and
video players with user controls

Add a favicon to the browser tab for
branding
prerequisites: Understanding of basic HTML structure (head, body, tags)
introduction:
Websites are not just static documents; they are a web of connected pages. In
this phase, we learn how to create 'Hyperlinks' to navigate the internet. We
will also explore how to add 'Media'—images, sounds, and videos—to make your
website visually appealing and interactive.

concept_explanation.anchor_tags:
The  (anchor) tag is used to create links. The most important
attribute is 'href', which stands for Hypertext Reference. This tells the
browser where to go when the link is clicked.
concept_explanation.relative_vs_absolute:
Absolute URLs are full addresses starting with 'https://' (used for external
sites). Relative URLs point to files within your own project folder (e.g.,
'about.html').
concept_explanation.images:
The  tag displays pictures. It is a 'void' tag,
meaning it doesn't have a closing tag. It requires 'src' (source path) and 'alt'
(alternate text for screen readers).
concept_explanation.media_elements:
The  and  tags allow
you to embed multimedia. You must include the 'controls' attribute so users can
play/pause.
concept_explanation.favicon:
A favicon is the small icon seen on the browser tab.
It is defined in the  using the  tag.

breakdown_of_syntax.href: The destination URL or file path.
breakdown_of_syntax.target_blank:
An attribute that tells the browser to open the link in a new
tab.
breakdown_of_syntax.src: The source path where the image or media file is stored.
breakdown_of_syntax.alt:
Text description of an image for accessibility and SEO; shown if the image
fails to load.
breakdown_of_syntax.controls:
A boolean attribute that adds play, pause, and
volume buttons to media.
breakdown_of_syntax.rel_icon:
In the favicon link, 'rel' defines the
relationship (icon) and 'href' specifies the icon file.

rules_and_key_points.rule_1:
Always provide 'alt' text for images to
ensure accessibility (A11y).
rules_and_key_points.rule_2:
when linking to
external websites to keep users on your site.
rules_and_key_points.rule_3:
Relative paths are
better for local files as they don't break if you move the site to a new
domain.
rules_and_key_points.rule_4:
Images should be in modern formats like WebP for better
performance.
rules_and_key_points.rule_5: The  tag is self-closing; do not use .
rules_and_key_points.rule_6:
Video and Audio tags need the 'controls' attribute, or they will be
invisible/unplayable.
rules_and_key_points.rule_7:
Place the favicon  inside the  section
only.
rules_and_key_points.rule_8:
SVG images are best for icons and logos because they don't
lose quality when scaled.
rules_and_key_points.rule_9:
Use 'width' and 'height' attributes on
images to prevent layout shifts during loading.

code_example_1.description:
A
simple example showing a link to Google and a local image.

code_example_2.title: Embedding Video and Audio
code_example_2.description:
How to add a video player
and an audio clip with playback controls.
real_world_use_cases:
Building a navigation bar to move between 'Home',
'About', and 'Contact' pages.

Adding a logo to the top of a webpage using the
 tag.
Embedding a product demo video on an e-commerce landing page.

Adding
a favicon so users can easily find your site among many open tabs.

Creating
'Download' links for PDF resumes or brochures.
common_mistakes: Forgetting the 'href' in an anchor tag, which makes it just plain text.
Using a backslash (\) instead of a forward slash (/) in file paths.

Missing
the 'alt' attribute, which is bad for SEO and blind users.

Forgetting the
'controls' attribute in audio/video, making the media unplayable.

Linking to
a local file on your computer (C:\Users\...) instead of a relative path.
best_practices:
Use descriptive 'alt' text: 'Red sports car' is better than
'image1'.

Always use the 'width' and 'height' attributes to reserve space for
images.

Organize your images in an 'images' or 'assets' folder for a clean
project structure.

Use the .webp format for images to keep your website
fast.

Always provide a fallback message inside video/audio tags for old
browsers.

faqs.question:
What happens if I don't put 'http://' in
an external link?
faqs.answer:
The browser will think it's a relative path and
look for a file named 'www.google.com' inside your own folder, which will result
in a 404 error.

faqs.question: Which image format is best for logos?
faqs.answer:
SVG (Scalable Vector Graphics) is best for logos because it remains
sharp at any size and has a small file size.

code_example_2.summary:
In this phase, we
learned how to make our web pages interactive and media-rich. We mastered the
anchor tag for navigation, the image tag for visuals, and the audio/video tags
for multimedia. We also learned how to properly path these files and the
importance of accessibility through alt text.
key_takeaways:
The  tag
creates hyperlinks via the 'href' attribute.

target='_blank' opens links in a
new window/tab.

Absolute URLs are for external sites; Relative URLs are for
local files.
The  tag is a void element that requires 'src' and 'alt'.

Alt
text is vital for accessibility and screen readers.

Audio and Video tags must
have the 'controls' attribute to be usable.

Multiple  tags can be used for
browser compatibility.
Favicons are added in the  using .

WebP and SVG are
preferred modern image formats.

Use forward slashes (/) for all paths in
HTML.

questions_and_answers.question:
What attribute is required to make a
link actually point to a destination?

Explain
the difference between src='image.jpg' and src='images/photo.jpg'.
questions_and_answers.answer:
The first one looks for 'image.jpg' in the same folder as the HTML file. The
second one looks inside a sub-folder named 'images' for a file named
'photo.jpg'.

questions_and_answers.question:
Write the code
for a link that says 'Visit Portfolio' and opens 'portfolio.html' in a new
tab.

Create an image element for
'logo.png' that is 100 pixels wide and has a descriptive text for the blind.

Write the code to embed an audio file named
'song.mp3' with controls and a fallback message for browsers that don't support
it.
questions_and_answers.answer:

 Your browser
does not support the audio element.
